subject: Geology
title: Common Metamorphic Rocks
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Common Metamorphic Rocks.

hook.text: Marble was once limestone. Slate was once shale. Quartzite was once sandstone. Every metamorphic rock has a parent.

buildup.text: Knowing the parent rock, or protolith, helps geologists understand the conditions that created the new rock.

discovery.text: Marble forms from calcite recrystallizing. Quartzite forms from sand grains fusing so tightly they won't split.

twist.text: Slate is so fine-grained and strong that it's been used for roofing and chalkboards for centuries.

climax.text: Each metamorphic rock tells a two-part story: what it was before and what forces transformed it.

punchline.text: Every metamorphic rock has a secret past.

quiz.question: What is the parent rock of marble?
